title: Sample Game Showcase
summary: Open a curated Game world with sample party members, map, journal, lore, and messages before configuring a model.
audience: New or returning users who want to feel how De-Koi works before setting up a provider.
where: Home > Explore sample world, or Discover > Sample Game Showcase.

title: Conversation Mode
summary: Casual character messaging with group chats, per-character bubble styling, schedules, statuses, autonomous replies, commands, cancellable message and draft translation, freshness guardrails, and familiar chat controls.
audience: Users who want companion-style chats, quick conversations, or character group messages.
where: Home > Conversation, then the chat setup wizard and chat settings drawer.

title: Roleplay Mode
summary: Long-form story writing with configurable narration style, cancellable message and draft translation, character narration, scene lifecycle controls, sprites, backgrounds, lorebooks, trackers, and optional agents.
audience: Users who want immersive scenes and creative writing rather than quick texting.
where: Home > Roleplay, then the setup wizard and chat settings drawer.

title: Game Mode
summary: A visual-novel and RPG-style mode with an AI Game Master, cancellable narration and draft translation, party, map, journal, inventory, combat, action-aware world tick recaps, and scene tools.
audience: Players who want the app to help run an adventure instead of only writing dialogue.
where: Home > Game, then the setup wizard and in-game control panel.

title: Characters
summary: Create, import, organize into groups, edit character cards, manage version history and character-level memories, choose cross-chat continuity, generate concrete card fields, tune Public Profile details, and save Music Taste for visible Listening To flavor, play cues, and shuffled profile music.
audience: Users building or collecting characters to chat or roleplay with.
where: Title bar > Characters; open a character, then use Memories for cross-chat continuity or version history for saved card versions.

title: Conversation Avatar
summary: Override a character's avatar in Conversation mode — show an emoji, one of its sprites, a gallery image, or hide it entirely.
audience: Users theming how a character looks next to their messages in Conversation mode.
where: Character editor > Colors tab > Conversation Avatar.

title: Personas
summary: Manage who you play as, including persona descriptions, avatars, and groups that chats can use for context.
audience: Users who want different identities or player characters for different chats.
where: Title bar > Personas, or chat settings for per-chat persona choices.

title: Lorebooks
summary: Store, organize, test, and inspect world info, setting details, rules, backstory, and keyword-triggered context assigned to chats, characters, or personas.
audience: Users with worlds, settings, factions, or recurring facts too large for a character card.
where: Title bar > Lorebooks for the test panel, or the chat Active World Info button for the inspector.

title: Prompt Presets
summary: Control and organize prompt structure, sections, variables, context attribution, and generation behavior for different chat styles.
audience: Power users tuning how models receive instructions and context.
where: Title bar > Presets, plus per-chat settings.

title: Regex Scripts
summary: Manage SillyTavern-style find/replace scripts that transform user input, AI output, or prompt-only text.
audience: Power users importing scoped scripts from cards or customizing text before it is sent, displayed, or assembled into prompts.
where: Title bar > Presets > Regexes, plus scoped regex controls in per-chat settings.

title: Connections
summary: Configure and organize model providers, API keys, local endpoints, managed Local Model setup, subscription image generation, voice providers, separate chat, agent, and Illustrator defaults, and per-chat vision connections for image input.
audience: Users setting up De-Koi, running a local model, grouping providers, or switching which model powers chats and agents.
where: Title bar > Connections, including connection folders and the Local Model card; choose a chat-specific Vision / Image Input Connection in Chat Settings.

title: Agents
summary: Optional helpers and custom functions that can be named, credited with editable author metadata, enabled or disabled globally, imported, exported, track world state, review or write lorebooks, guide prose, create images, run custom behavior, and enrich generation.
audience: Users who want extra systems layered on top of their main chat.
where: Title bar > Agents, Title bar > Presets > Functions, and each chat settings drawer.

title: Settings
summary: Global controls for appearance, chat behavior, sound preferences, imports, themes, extensions, backups, updates, and advanced runtime options.
audience: Everyone configuring how the app looks, behaves, imports data, or connects to runtimes.
where: Title bar > Settings.

title: Privacy And Data Controls
summary: Review how De-Koi stores and sends data, export or back up your library, erase selected categories, or perform a typed-confirmation complete wipe of De-Koi-managed data.
audience: Users who want to understand De-Koi's data handling, remove selected data, or permanently erase every De-Koi-managed copy.
where: Settings > Privacy & Data.

title: Health And Diagnostics
summary: Review setup readiness, runtime health, Local Model status, provider probes, storage reachability, recent client diagnostics, and a redacted troubleshooting packet.
audience: Users checking first-run setup or collecting safe troubleshooting details for support.
where: Settings > Health.

title: Notification Sounds
summary: Choose the Frog default, the Legacy ding, or a small custom audio file for local chat alerts.
audience: Users who want Conversation and Roleplay alerts to sound different or match older Marinara builds.
where: Settings > Appearance > Notifications.

title: Imports
summary: Bring in characters, personas, lorebooks, presets, SillyTavern data, and Marinara profile exports.
audience: Users migrating from another frontend or restoring existing Marinara data.
where: Settings > Import.

title: Bot Browser
summary: Browse downloadable character cards and related community resources from inside De-Koi.
audience: Users looking for characters and resources to start with.
where: Title bar > Browser.

title: Help Hub
summary: A central Help surface for diagnostics, bug reports, onboarding replay, shortcut reference, and app version details.
audience: Users who are stuck, confused, or want one place to find recovery actions.
where: Title bar Help button, mobile chat Help button, or press ? outside text inputs.

title: Show Me Around
summary: Replay the short five-step orientation tour for Chats, workspace navigation, and the main workspace. Use the readiness checklist to resume setup without changing your library or settings.
audience: Users who skipped onboarding or want the initial orientation again.
where: Discover > Show Me Around, or Help > Show me around.

title: Report A Bug
summary: Copy a short support report and open the configured GitHub issue form.
audience: Users who hit an error or need to send maintainers useful troubleshooting context.
where: Crash screen, Settings > Health, Help Hub, or Discover > Report A Bug.

title: Deki-senpai
summary: A dedicated Deki-senpai surface for app questions, codebase help, creative-library drafts with approval cards, durable retryable sessions, a history sidebar, and guided changes separate from active chats.
audience: Users who want help understanding, customizing, or drafting De-Koi library records without interrupting a chat.
where: Deki-senpai button in the title bar or mobile top bar, with conversation history in the dedicated left sidebar.

title: Game Tutorial
summary: A first-game spotlight tutorial for the Game map, party, controls, and narration/input area.
audience: Game Mode users who need a quick orientation after setup.
where: Auto-opens on the first game, and can be reopened from the Game controls help button.

title: Slash Commands
summary: Type / in the chat input to see command autocomplete for actions such as help, dice rolls, reminders, guided replies, response amendments, and impersonation.
audience: Users who prefer quick typed actions instead of hunting through menus.
where: Conversation and Roleplay chat input; type / to open autocomplete.

title: Text-to-Speech
summary: Configure voice providers and let chats, narration, or characters speak with assigned voices.
audience: Users who want voiced messages, narration, or more immersive scenes.
where: Settings and connection/provider configuration, with chat-specific voice options where available.

title: Image Generation
summary: Set up and test image connections, Codex Subscription imagegen, and style profiles for selfies, sprites, backgrounds, NPC portraits, and Game scene illustrations.
audience: Users who want visual scenes and character art generated from chat context.
where: Connections, Settings > General, chat settings, Game controls, and relevant agents.

title: Trackers
summary: World, persona, character, quest, and custom trackers summarize structured state that can appear in Roleplay and Game surfaces.
audience: Users who want visible continuity and structured state during long scenes.
where: Enable tracker agents in chat settings, then use the tracker side panel or HUD surfaces.

title: Discord Mirror
summary: Mirror chat messages into a Discord channel through a webhook while keeping character names and narration labels readable.
audience: Users who want to archive or share chat output outside De-Koi.
where: Per-chat settings drawer.

title: Inline Model Reasoning
summary: Optionally show reasoning returned by the model in a separate collapsible panel beneath character replies while keeping final answers primary.
audience: Users who want to inspect reasoning data that their selected model and provider make available.
where: Open a Conversation or Roleplay chat, then Chat Settings > Reasoning.

title: Prompt Overrides
summary: Customize selected prompt templates for specialized generation behavior without editing core source.
audience: Power users tuning advanced prompts for chat, images, and helper behavior.
where: Settings > Advanced.

title: Quick Replies
summary: Optional input actions for posting without generation, guiding a reply, impersonating a character, or saving custom slash-command buttons from mode input toolbars.
audience: Users who want faster alternate send actions during writing.
where: Settings > General > Chat behavior, then the Conversation, Roleplay, or Game input toolbar.

title: Music Player
summary: Generate a fresh pick from the visible Music Player controls, or activate the Music Player agent for automatic scene-aware picks and Game Mode scene-music retries. Character Music Taste supplies profile and manual character cues.
audience: Users who want music controls, smarter roleplay cues, or mood-matched playlists inside chats and games.
where: Chat or Game settings > Agents for automatic Music Player picks, Settings > Modules for visible controls, and Character editor > Metadata > Music Taste for profile cues.

title: Chat Settings Presets
summary: Save, apply, import, export, rename, and delete reusable chat settings bundles for repeated chat setups.
audience: Users who regularly create similar chats and want setup choices to carry across sessions.
where: Open a chat > Chat Settings > preset controls at the top of the drawer.

title: Prompt Inspector
summary: Inspect advanced prompt details including budget estimates, context attribution, section token counts, warnings, assembled prompt sections, roles, variables, generation parameters, and replay details before or after generation.
audience: Power users checking context usage before sending or debugging why a model answered a certain way.
where: Assistant message Peek Prompt actions, context attribution controls, replay controls, and the Game More menu.

title: Swipes And Rerolls
summary: Move between alternate generations, regenerate replies, amend the latest response with a targeted instruction, and optionally use your draft as guidance for the next retry.
audience: Writers who compare alternate responses or steer a regeneration without changing the whole chat setup.
where: Assistant messages in Conversation and Roleplay chats, with related controls in Settings > General and Settings > Advanced.

title: Message Moment Actions
summary: Start from a chat message and route it to continuity actions, including remembering the beat in chat summaries, drafting lorebook entries, or adding Game journal notes and checkpoints when supported. Use the brain action for Remember this, the adjacent branch action for branching, and the paintbrush to illustrate that exact message.
audience: Users who notice an important chat beat and want to preserve it as summary memory, lore, Game notes, checkpoints, a branch, or a targeted illustration from the message action bar.
where: Hover or tap a message action bar in Conversation, Roleplay, or Game transcripts, then choose the brain for Remember this, the branch icon to branch, or the paintbrush to illustrate that exact message.

title: Game Journal
summary: Browse auto-tracked Game Mode notes for events, NPCs, locations, inventory, world tick recaps, and campaign continuity.
audience: Game Mode players who need a readable record of what has happened and what the world contains.
where: Game Mode toolbar > Journal.

title: Game Checkpoints
summary: Review, load, branch, and manage Game Mode checkpoints created around session boundaries and major state transitions.
audience: Game Mode players who want recovery points before risky scenes, combat, or long sessions.
where: Game Mode toolbar > Checkpoints.

title: Game Gallery And Assets
summary: Use generated scene images, galleries, sprites, backgrounds, music, ambient tracks, sound effects, and managed game assets.
audience: Game Mode players and builders who want visual scenes, reusable media, and audio-backed sessions.
where: Game Mode toolbar > Gallery or Game Assets, and Settings > General for asset scanning and image defaults.

title: Game Combat And Session Tools
summary: Manage combat turns, combat logs, skill checks, interruptions, new sessions, session ending, and Game Mode history.
audience: Game Mode players who want to understand the action tools around adventures and combat.
where: Game Mode toolbar and Game actions menu during an active game.

title: Character Gallery
summary: Store and review character-specific images separately from chat gallery images and generated scene attachments.
audience: Users who keep multiple reference images, portraits, or art variants for the same character.
where: Characters > open a character > Gallery.

title: Gallery
summary: Keep a profile-wide image library organized into folders, reusable across the whole app and independent of any chat.
audience: Users who want a central place to collect and organize images instead of scattering them across chats.
where: Gallery button in the right-side panel navigation.

title: Modules
summary: Enable or disable bundled modules like ME Notes and Music Player without importing custom extension files.
audience: Users who want opt-in De-Koi features that are shipped with the app and can be toggled safely.
where: Settings > Modules.

title: Extensions
summary: Install CSS, JavaScript, or package manifest extensions, then enable, disable, and manage local behavior without editing core source.
audience: Users customizing De-Koi with optional behavior that should stay outside core app code.
where: Settings > Extensions.

title: Extension Device Access
summary: Review compatibility and requested helpers, then activate CSS or trusted page-level JavaScript only on this runtime and device.
audience: Users reviewing whether an installed extension should run on this device.
where: Settings > Extensions > Device access.

title: Customization Safe Mode
summary: Recover from a broken theme or extension without loading customizations by opening De-Koi with ?safe-mode=customizations.
audience: Users whose custom CSS or extension made the normal interface difficult to use.
where: Append ?safe-mode=customizations to the De-Koi URL, or review Settings > Themes before a problem occurs.

title: Backups And Profile Export
summary: Create managed backups, download backup files, export a native profile JSON, and restore data through profile import.
audience: Users moving devices, testing risky changes, or keeping recovery copies of local data.
where: Settings > Import for profile import, Settings > Privacy & Data for the privacy overview, and Settings > Advanced for backup and profile export controls.

title: Themes, Fonts, And Backgrounds
summary: Customize visual themes, custom CSS themes, fonts, chat backgrounds, game asset backgrounds, gradient styling, and mode-specific appearance.
audience: Users who want De-Koi to look or read differently for their setup, screenshots, or accessibility needs.
where: Settings > Appearance and Settings > Themes.

title: Custom Font Upload
summary: Install validated TTF, OTF, WOFF, or WOFF2 fonts from Settings; remote runtimes upload while embedded apps can open the managed folder.
audience: Users tailoring typography on embedded or remote De-Koi runtimes.
where: Settings > Appearance > Font.

title: Roleplay Context Panels
summary: Use roleplay helper panels for author notes, secret plot, context injections, active world info, sprites, and HUD state.
audience: Roleplay users who want more control over hidden context, visible state, and scene presentation.
where: Roleplay chat HUD, chat settings, and agent-enabled roleplay panels.

title: Chat Continuity Overview
summary: See what can carry forward: chat-local transcript fragments, inherited character-wide memories, typed consequences from saved exchanges, automatic capture status and partial-capture warnings, and embeddings that rank matches, alongside summaries, world info, and tracker agents.
audience: Users running long conversations, roleplays, or campaigns who want to know what context can carry forward.
where: Open a chat > Chat Settings > Continuity for chat-local and inherited character-wide memory details; open a remembered message chip for exact saved consequences or a partial-capture warning; control automatic memory-save notifications in Settings > Appearance > Notifications.

title: Character Web Research
summary: Let characters decide when current facts need public-web research. Keep the process in the background or allow in-character narration, with permission prompts and collapsed final sources.
audience: Conversation and roleplay users who want characters to check current public information.
where: Open a Conversation or Roleplay chat > Chat Settings > Character Web Research.
